original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
original_registry_entry.version: NICE NG115; updated 2019-07 with current links reviewed 2025-03
original_registry_entry.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for adult COPD follow-up and exacerbation-feature documentation; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.3.1 — COPD exacerbation features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
